stem: A CPU uses several special-purpose registers during the fetch–decode–execute cycle, including the Program Counter (PC), the Memory Address Register (MAR), the Memory Data Register (MDR) and the Accumulator (ACC).
model_answer: PC: holds the address of the next instruction to be fetched. MAR: holds the memory address currently being read from or written to. MDR: holds the data that is being transferred between the CPU and memory. Accumulator: holds the results of calculations performed by the ALU.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: State the purpose of each of the following registers: PC, MAR, MDR, Accumulator.

mark_points.text: PC: holds the address of the next instruction to be fetched
MAR: holds the memory address currently being accessed
MDR: holds data being transferred between the CPU and memory
accepted_alternatives: MDR: data read from or written to memory

mark_points.text: Accumulator: holds the result of a calculation performed by the ALU
accepted_alternatives: ACC: stores the result of ALU operations
